query.title: IMAP Search Query
query.description: IMAP search query (used with IMAP connection method). Find more information on the syntax in the documentation.

graph_filter.title: Graph API Filter
graph_filter.description: OData $filter expression (used with Graph API connection method). Can be combined with Period from date. Leave empty to fetch all messages. See Microsoft Graph API docs for syntax.

options.tooltip: Supports exact matches with eq, e.g. from/emailAddress/address eq 'someone@example.com' or subject eq 'exact subject' or hasAttachments eq true. Note: contains() on subject/body is NOT supported and will return HTTP 400.

graph_search.title: Graph API Search (KQL)
graph_search.description: KQL search expression for the Graph API (used with Graph API connection method). Cannot be combined with Graph API Filter or Period from date. As per Microsoft Graph known issues: "Query parameters specified in a request might fail silently. This can be true for unsupported query parameters and for unsupported combinations of query parameters." Returns up to 1,000 results.

options.tooltip: Keyword search: subject:weekly, from:someone@example.com (substring match). Exact phrase: subject:"my exact phrase". Date range: received:2026-01-01..2026-01-31. Combine all: from:sender subject:keyword received:2026-01-01..2026-03-18. Note: relative dates (received:this week) do NOT work.

imap_folder.title: Mail Folder
imap_folder.description: Folder to get the emails from. Defaults to INBOX. For IMAP: use folder path (e.g. INBOX, Sent Items). For Graph API: use well-known folder names (inbox, sentitems, drafts, deleteditems) or folder display name. If left empty, default Inbox folder is used.

date_since.title: Period from date (including)
date_since.description: Date in YYYY-MM-DD format or dateparser string i.e. 5 days ago, 1 month ago, yesterday, etc. If left empty, all records are downloaded. Cannot be combined with Graph API Search — use received:YYYY-MM-DD..YYYY-MM-DD date syntax within the KQL expression instead.

download_content.title: Download Content
download_content.description: When set to true, the content of the message will be downloaded.

download_attachments.title: Download Attachments
download_attachments.description: When set to true, also the attachments will be downloaded. By default into the File Storage. Use processors to control the behaviour.

mark_seen.title: Mark seen
mark_seen.description: When set to true, emails that have been extracted will be marked as seen/read.

attachment_pattern.title: Attachment Pattern
attachment_pattern.description: Regex pattern to filter particular attachments. e.g. to retrieve only pdf file types use: .+\.pdf If left empty, all attachments are downloaded.
